100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 8, 1926, the United States Senate voted to approve the construction of a new naval base in the Philippines, signaling a commitment to expanding American military presence in the Pacific. Meanwhile, the British Empire faced growing tensions in India as protests against colonial rule intensified, with leaders advocating for greater autonomy. In the realm of science, a notable discovery was made in the field of physics as scientists reported advancements in understanding atomic structure, hinting at future technological innovations.
